title: Richly Grilled Malaysian Chicken Satay
description: A classic Malaysian dish featuring grilled chicken in a mixture of spices and citrus, served with a side of velvety peanut sauce. This low-carb-friendly recipe is perfect for a light dinner or snack.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, palm sugar, fish sauce, lime juice, and coconut milk to make the marinade.
Add the chicken strips to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Thread the marinated chicken strips onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill or grill pan with peanut oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the chicken satay for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through and slightly charred.
Serve the grilled chicken satay hot with a side of peanut sauce, sliced cucumbers, and ketupat or rice.
To make the peanut sauce, combine peanut butter, coconut milk, and lime juice in a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Season the peanut sauce with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish the peanut sauce with chopped peanuts and cilantro, if desired.
